title: Agent-Friendly CLI Bridge Pattern
summary: A pattern for building CLI tools designed to be called by AI agents rather than humans. Key design decisions: (1) use stdlib only (urllib, json, argparse) for zero-install portability, (2) call /health before /brief to validate API availability with a clear error message including the exact startup command, (3) output structured text with labeled sections that agents can parse, (4) provide --json flag for machine-readable output, (5) include motion asset bootstrap as an automatic secondary search for high-end UI tasks, (6) handle encoding for Windows consoles (reconfigure stdout/stderr to UTF-8).
why_reusable: AI agents need CLI tools that are reliable, self-diagnosing, and produce parseable output. This pattern of health-check-first, clear error messages with fix instructions, dual human/JSON output, and stdlib-only portability generalizes to any API-backed CLI tool meant for agent consumption.
adapter_target: Generic agent CLI template — extract the health-check-first pattern, error message format, and output structure into a reusable CLI builder.
implementation_notes: Health-check-first: call /health before the main endpoint; if unreachable, print startup command and exit 2
Stdlib-only: urllib.request, json, argparse — no pip install needed for agents to use
Dual output: human-readable by default (labeled sections, bullet points), --json for machine parsing
Encoding: reconfigure stdout/stderr to UTF-8 on Windows to handle CJK characters
Motion asset bootstrap: automatically run additional /assets/search queries for high-end UI tasks
Error message tells the agent exactly how to start the API — actionable, not just error description
tests_required: Unit test: health check failure returns exit code 2 with startup command
Unit test: --json flag outputs valid JSON
Unit test: motion asset bootstrap triggers on matching task terms
Integration test: successful brief call prints all expected sections
Integration test: API unreachable → clear error, no silent fallback
risks: Stdlib urllib is less ergonomic than requests/httpx — error handling is more verbose
Windows console encoding (GBK) may still cause issues despite UTF-8 reconfiguration
Argument defaults may drift from API defaults if not kept in sync
Motion asset bootstrap adds latency (9 extra API calls) — should be opt-in for non-UI tasks
do_not_do: Do not silently fall back to GitHub or file reading when the API is unreachable
Do not require pip install for the CLI — agents may not have permission
Do not print raw stack traces to agents — format errors as actionable messages
dependencies: none (stdlib only: urllib, json, argparse, textwrap, sys)
notes: Extracted from DataBase scripts/brief.py (343 lines). The health-check-first, dual output, error-with-fix-instructions pattern is the reusable part.
